partner.name: Moritz Baier-Lentz
partner.title: Partner, Head of Gaming & Interactive Media

gaming_ai_thesis: Lightspeed Gaming & Interactive Media invests at the intersection of gaming and artificial intelligence, with a specific focus on foundation models for spatiotemporal reasoning (action policies and world models), generative media creation, and agentic simulations in video games. Led by Moritz Baier-Lentz, a former professional Diablo II player (global #1 ranking), the team brings deep gaming operational expertise combined with technical depth in AI infrastructure. Their thesis centers on gaming-born technology fueling broader AI applications—from GPUs to real-time engines to synthetic environments. They actively invest in AI-native game development platforms, dynamic world generation, and intelligent NPC systems.
geographic_focus: Global (13 offices across US, Europe, Asia)
notable_gaming_investments: Inworld AI (AI character engine, Series B in under 2 years)
recent_fund: Gaming & Interactive Media pool from $10B+ AI funds (Dec 2025)
outreach_angle: Moritz is a literal gaming pro who traded virtual items for college tuition—he deeply understands AI's potential in gaming. Lightspeed's portfolio including Epic Games, Stability AI, and Inworld AI demonstrates their conviction in AI-gaming intersections. Their 'Gaming x AI Market Map' research shows active thought leadership. Moritz's background at BITKRAFT before Lightspeed gives him unique perspective on synthetic reality and AI infrastructure.

gaming_ai_thesis: 359 Capital (formerly Sapphire Sport) pursues a distinct strategy focused exclusively on early to mid-stage consumer and consumer-adjacent opportunities, with gaming and AI Applications as two of their seven primary focus areas. Their unique LP base includes iconic brands across sports, media, and entertainment (City Football Group, adidas, AEG, MSG, Sinclair) providing portfolio companies with unparalleled distribution access. Their thesis on AI in gaming centers on next-generation game engines, AI-powered content creation tools, and intelligent game infrastructure. They recently backed Jabali, an AI-native game engine enabling no-code game development.
outreach_angle: 359 Capital's recent investment in Jabali (AI game engine) demonstrates explicit conviction in AI-gaming infrastructure. Their unique LP network of sports/media/entertainment brands provides distribution advantages for AI-powered interactive content. Michael Spirito's quote—'helping founders do the impossible'—aligns with the ambitious NEXUS AI vision. The spinout from Sapphire gives them fresh capital and autonomy to pursue gaming+AI aggressively.

gaming_ai_thesis: Play Ventures is the leading gaming and consumer apps VC, with a strong emphasis on B2B, AI & Infrastructure as one of their three core investment pillars. Their newly raised $140M Fund II (December 2025) explicitly targets 'AI-enhanced gaming tools' alongside mobile free-to-play games and gaming infrastructure. They believe hyper-personalization, continuous optimization, and frictionless transactions driven by AI are transforming how games attract, retain, and monetize users. Their portfolio includes AI-powered companies like Scenario (AI game assets) and Appcharge (mobile payments infrastructure). They actively seek founders building products with 'unprecedented speed and impact' through AI.
notable_gaming_investments: Bigger Games (Istanbul studio, Index Ventures co-invest)
outreach_angle: Play Ventures' freshly raised $140M Fund II (Dec 2025) is actively deploying in AI-enhanced gaming. Harri co-founded Rocket Pack (acquired by Disney) and Matchmade, giving him deep operational empathy. Their 'B2B, AI & Infrastructure' focus directly aligns with NEXUS AI's platform approach. Fund I returned 1.5x DPI in under 4 years—proven track record of supporting founders through to exit.

gaming_ai_thesis: Vgames is Israel's first and leading venture fund dedicated exclusively to gaming, founded by Eitan Reisel who previously built and led the Google Games team in Tel Aviv. Their thesis centers on supporting founders through their entire lifecycle from early days to growth, with particular conviction in data-driven game development and AI-powered gaming experiences. They believe the combination of data and art is what makes a great game—and increasingly, AI is the bridge between these disciplines. They offer non-dilutive growth financing alongside traditional equity investments, giving founders flexibility in capital structure. Their focus on gaming-native founders and deep publisher relationships provides unique distribution advantages.
notable_gaming_investments: Sett (AI-powered storytelling for mobile games, $15M Series A with Bessemer)
Vgames Growth Financing portfolio (non-dilutive capital for UA)
outreach_angle: Vgames' investment in Sett (AI-powered storytelling) demonstrates their conviction in AI-gaming intersections. Eitan's background leading Google Games gives him unique perspective on platform-scale gaming infrastructure. The non-dilutive growth financing option provides founders flexibility—relevant for AI infrastructure plays with different capital needs. Fresh $82M Fund II (Dec 2024) means active deployment.

gaming_ai_thesis: The Games Fund (TGF) is the most active early-stage VC fund in the gaming industry, named one of the top funds of 2024. Founded by industry veterans Ilya Eremeev and Maria Kochmola (formerly MGVC/MY.GAMES) along with GEM Capital and ex-Riot CTO Sergey Titov, they bring deep operational and investment expertise. Their thesis centers on next-generation gaming infrastructure, including AI-powered development tools, procedural content generation, and AI-native game engines. They have strong conviction in AI's ability to democratize game creation and enable entirely new gameplay experiences. Their portfolio includes Layer AI (AI game art tools) and multiple AI-native gaming startups.
notable_gaming_investments: Layer AI (AI game art tools, $6.5M seed)
Eschatology Entertainment ($11.3M round led by Krafton)
outreach_angle: TGF was named the most active early-stage gaming VC in 2024—their finger is on the pulse. Ilya and Maria's backgrounds at MGVC (MY.GAMES corporate venture arm) gave them deep gaming investment experience. Their investment in Layer AI demonstrates explicit AI-gaming thesis. The fund's outsized raise ($48M vs $30M target) shows LP conviction. Being named a top fund of 2024 means they're actively deploying and winning competitive deals.
